judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent built the set of UC-assigned patents only from records where Patents_info LIKE '%UNIV CALIFORNIA%' (var_function-call-7667597160845419365) and then matched citations against that subset, missing other UC assignee variants (e.g., "THE REGENTS OF UNIV OF CALIFORNIA"), which led to returning only three citing assignees and incomplete CPC titles.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: yes
FM4: no
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python (function-call-5271790204502772206), the agent implemented brittle parsing and exact-string matching—extracting UC publication numbers via a regex on Patents_info and requiring exact equality with citation publication_number, plus narrow regexes for assignee and subclass extraction—so it only found four citing assignees and returned an incomplete set compared to the ground truth.

B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
Although the agent correctly gathered UC publication numbers and queried citing records, its execute_python step (var_function-call-17200647311021866077) used overly restrictive regex logic to extract assignees from Patents_info and thus returned only four assignee–subclass pairs, missing many valid citing assignees present in the query results and required by the ground truth.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python (function-call-16476090751936723263), the agent relied on brittle parsing—building a small set of UC publication numbers from Patents_info via a narrow regex and matching citations only by exact publication_number equality, then taking cpc_list[0][:4] as the “primary” subclass—yielding only three citing assignees and missing many valid results.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no

FM3: no
FM4: yes

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the key execute_python step (function-call-4891104776307271307), the agent incorrectly took CPC subclasses from the citing patents' "cpc" field rather than from the cited UNIV CALIFORNIA patents, so the reported titles correspond to the wrong patents and the result set is incomplete.
B. Failure modes encountered:
